---
title: "Software"
---

This page lists every tool the workshop assumes is installed on your laptop. If you'd rather run a single copy-pasteable installation script that does it all in one go, jump to **[Setup](Setup.qmd)**.

## Required tools

-   **R ≥ 4.4** --- [download](https://www.r-project.org)
-   **RStudio Desktop** (current release) --- [download](https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/)
-   **A terminal that supports `ssh`** (for Talapas; see below for Windows users)
-   **Git ≥ 2.30** --- [download](https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2/Getting-Started-Installing-Git)
-   **A GitHub account** --- [sign up](https://github.com/signup)
-   *(Optional but recommended)* **VS Code** for editing files on Talapas via SSH --- see the [VS Code & Talapas](Resources_Folder/VSCode_Talapas.html) reference

## Required R / Bioconductor packages

The full set used across the lectures, tutorials, and bonus exercise. Install everything in one session.

``` r
# 1. CRAN
install.packages(c(
  "tidyverse",     # dplyr, ggplot2, tidyr, readr, purrr, tibble, stringr, forcats
  "Seurat",        # core scRNA-seq workflow (workshop default)
  "remotes",       # GitHub installs
  "BiocManager",   # Bioconductor installer
  "patchwork",     # combining ggplots
  "presto",        # fast Wilcoxon for FindMarkers (recommended)
  "harmony",       # batch integration
  "clustree",      # picking clustering resolution
  "ggrepel"        # readable labels on UMAPs
))

# 2. Bioconductor
if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("BiocManager")
BiocManager::install(c(
  "SingleCellExperiment",
  "scran",
  "scater",
  "scuttle",
  "DropletUtils",      # EmptyDrops, knee-point cell calling
  "scDblFinder",       # doublet detection
  "DESeq2",            # pseudobulk DE
  "edgeR",             # alt. pseudobulk DE
  "limma",             # alt. pseudobulk DE
  "fgsea",             # rank-based gene-set enrichment
  "clusterProfiler",   # over-representation + GSEA
  "SingleR",           # automatic annotation
  "celldex",           # reference datasets for SingleR
  "Signac",            # scATAC-seq (bonus exercise)
  "EnsDb.Hsapiens.v86" # genome annotation for Signac
))

# 3. GitHub (one common dependency)
remotes::install_github("satijalab/seurat-data", quiet = TRUE)
```

::: callout-note
The first install will take 20–60 minutes depending on your machine and whether your platform is building from source. Run it before the workshop, not the morning of.
:::

## Verify your install

After installation, run this once to confirm everything loads. If any line errors, re-install just that package.

``` r
pkgs <- c("Seurat","SingleCellExperiment","scran","scater","DropletUtils",
          "scDblFinder","harmony","DESeq2","fgsea","clusterProfiler",
          "SingleR","celldex","Signac","tidyverse","clustree","presto")
ok <- vapply(pkgs, requireNamespace, logical(1), quietly = TRUE)
cat("Loaded:\n"); print(ok)
sessionInfo()
```

A clean `sessionInfo()` plus all `TRUE` values means you're set.

## Accessing the shell via a terminal application

### Mac or Linux

-   Use the built-in **Terminal** app, or install [iTerm2](https://iterm2.com/) (Mac) for a nicer experience.

### Windows --- install WSL2 + Ubuntu

The workshop assumes a POSIX shell. The simplest path on Windows is WSL2.

1.  Run **PowerShell as Administrator**.
2.  Type `wsl --install -d ubuntu` and press Enter. Restart when prompted.
3.  Launch **Ubuntu** from the Start menu, set a username and password (does not need to match your Windows login).
4.  Run:

    ``` bash
    sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade -y
    sudo apt install -y openssh-client git
    ```

Full Microsoft guide: <https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/wsl/install>

## Talapas (UO HPC) account --- optional

Required only if you intend to run anything on the cluster. Request an account at <https://racs.uoregon.edu/request-access>; once you have one, follow the [VS Code & Talapas](Resources_Folder/VSCode_Talapas.html) reference and the [SLURM Basics tutorial](Exercise_Folder/Tutorial_SLURM_Basics.html).

## Trouble with installs?

Check the [FAQ](FAQ.qmd) first. Common issues (Mac compiler errors, Bioconductor mirror, RStudio cache) are documented there.
